## Cross-Enrollment – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/cross-enrollment/
**Chunk 0**

Cross-Enrollment – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Cross-Enrollment
Home
/
Cross-Enrollment
Cross-Enrollment
superadmin
2026-04-06T05:35:03+00:00
Cross-Enrollment / Cross-Registration
Cross-enrollment / Cross-registration refers to enrollment in another academic unit outside the home unit. The enrollment of cross-registrants is subject to the approval of the Deans through the Faculty/College Secretaries of the home unit and the accepting faculty/colleges. Cross-Enrollment Requirements and Procedures:
Cross-Enrollment from UPOU to other UP Unit or Schools outside the UP System
Requirement:
Accomplished
Permit to Cross Register Form
. Procedure:
Step 1:
Submit the accomplished Permit to Cross Register Form to the Faculty of Study (FEd, FICS, or FMDS). Step 2:
Wait for an email from the Office of the University Registrar for the result. Step 3
: Secure the approval of the Dean and Registrar of the accepting UP unit. Step 4
: Complete the enrollment in the accepting unit during the prescribed registration period. Step 5:
Submit a copy of the final certificate of registration form (UP Form5 Dean’s copy) to the Office of the University Registrar once registered:
The Registration Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031 Philippines
Cross-Enrollment from other UP Units to UPOU
Requirements:
Accomplished UPOU cross-enrollment application form (
Google form Link
). Accomplished Permit to Cross Register Form from the student’s home UP unit, form must show approval of home unit’s Dean and Registrar. Note: List of courses offered for the term is available at
Course Offerings
page. Procedure:
Step 1
: Student secures the necessary permit/s  or document/s from his/her home UP unit to cross enroll to UPOU. Step 2
: Accomplish the UPOU cross-enrollment online application form via
Google Form Link
. Upload the duly signed cross-enrollment application from your home UP campus to the online application for enrollment form together with applicable supporting document/s (free tuition, TFE/Reduced fee, etc). Step 3
: The UPOU Registration Section forwards the printed application to the Faculty of Study (FOS) concerned for evaluation. Kindly note that the
Request for Cross-Enrollment Form is also subject to the approval of the UPOU Faculty Dean concerned as per recommendation of the Program Chair. Step 4
: The FOS sends back the evaluated application to the UPOU Registration Section (
[email protected]
). Step 5. The UPOU Registration Section emails the student about the status of cross-enrollment application. The email for approved applications shall include details on how to do the enrollment online. Step 6
: Student with approved cross-enrollment application enrolls in UPOU through the Online Registration System (ORS) lodged in the Student Portal. Once enrolled, the student automatically receives his/her access credentials to the Student Portal through his/her registered email address
Step 7
: Student logs in to his/her Student Portal account to view if his/her enrollment for the current term has already been shown and if the enrolled courses are all correct. Step 8
: The UPOU TechSupport emails cross-enrollee the log-in credentials for the UPOU MyPortal (UPOU’s online classroom) at the start of the class. Course site normally opens on the first day of classes. In case you cannot access the course site, email
MyPortal Techsupport at
[email protected]
for assistance. Cross-Enrollment from Schools outside the UP System to UPOU
Requirement:
Accomplished Permit to Cross Register Form from the mother unit duly approved by the Dean, Registrar , and Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs of the mother unit. The student’s email address must be indicated in the form.

Application for Dropping
superadmin
2019-10-28T03:36:22+00:00
Application for Dropping of Courses
A student may, with the consent of the instructor and the Faculty Dean, drop a course by filling out the prescribed form before three-fourths (3/4) of the semester/term has elapsed, and not later. The registration privilege of a student who drops a course without the approval of the Dean may be curtailed or entirely withdrawn. If a course is dropped after the middle of the term, the faculty member concerned shall indicate in the prescribed form the class standing of the student at the time of dropping as either Passing or Failing solely for administrative guidance. The application for dropping is done through the Student Portal. Application period begins at the start of classes and ends a day after the deadline for filing of application for dropping (refer to the
academic calendar
for deadlines). The student is required to pay the application for dropping fee of Php 10.00 per unit and upload the proof of payment in the Student Portal. See
procedures for payment of fees
. How to apply for Dropping of Courses:
Step 1
: Log in to the
Student Portal
and apply for dropping of courses. Step 2
: Upload the proof of payment to the system.

2025-01-02T08:35:19+00:00
Application for Graduation
The “No application”, “No graduation” policy is strictly implemented. The application for graduation serves as the basis for evaluating the record of the student/candidate. If the student is completing all the academic requirements of the program on his/her last enrollment, s/he should apply for graduation on or before the deadline for filing of application for graduation on that particular term. Please refer to the
academic calendar
for the deadline for filing the application for graduation. For more information about the UPOU Commencement Exercises and the graduation checklist, visit the
Graduation page
. Application Requirement:
Accomplished
Application Form for Graduation
.

---

## Application for Honorable Dismissal – Office of the University Registrar

**Chunk 0**

Home
/
Application for Honorable Dismissal
Application for Honorable Dismissal
superadmin
2025-09-29T06:18:32+00:00
Application for Honorable Dismissal
An Honorable Dismissal (HD) is specifically granted to a student who voluntarily withdraws from the University while maintaining good standing in terms of character and conduct. It serves as a certification that the student has no pending accountabilities with the University. How to Apply for Honorable Dismissal:
Step 1. Log in to the
Student Portal
and apply for
University Clearance
. Select
“Honorable Dismissal”
as the reason for clearing. Attach the following documents:
Signed letter addressed to the
University Registrar
stating the reason(s) for withdrawing from the program
Valid government-issued ID
Step 2. Wait for the
email notification
confirming whether your clearance has been approved. Step 3. Once the
University Clearance
is approved, you may request
Honorable Dismissal
and monitor the status of your request via
AIMS
. Note:
A student who has been granted an
Honorable Dismissal
may no longer re-enroll in their previous program.

---

superadmin
2019-12-17T02:49:57+00:00
Application for Leave of Absence
A student who is granted official leave of absence will be given a final grade of DRP for the courses he/she is currently enrolled in, with LOA under the Remarks column. The LOA must not exceed one year from the term of effectivity. A student who is on AWOL is not allowed to apply for LOA, he/she is advised to apply first for readmission. The application for leave of absence (LOA) is done through the Student Portal. Application begins after the deadline for the withdrawal of enrollment and ends a day after the deadline for filing of application for dropping (refer to the
academic calendar
for deadlines). The student is required to pay the application for LOA fee of PhP 150.00 and upload the proof of payment in the Student Portal. See
procedures for payment of fees
. How to file for Leave of Absence:
Step 1
: Log in to the
Student Portal
and apply for leave of absence. Step 2
: Upload the proof of payment to the system.

---

/

Application for Non-Degree
superadmin
2026-04-06T05:25:28+00:00
Application for Non-Degree
A non-degree student is one who is enrolled in certain courses but does not follow an organized program of study and, therefore not a prospective candidate for graduation for any degree in the University. Enrollment of non-degree student must be endorsed by the program chair and approved by the Faculty Dean. Application Requirements:
Application
F
orm for Non-Degree Students
;
Photocopy of Transcript of Records from all schools attended;
Two recent identical 2” x 2” photos and two identical 1” x 1” photos, with your name and the program applied for printed at the back of the photos. How to apply as non-degree student:
Step 1
: Check the
list of course offerings
posted at the UPOU website and completely fill-out the application form for non-degree. Make sure that course/s listed in the application form is/are offered. Step 2
: Mail or bring all application requirements at least one month before the registration schedule to:
The Registration Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031 Philippines
or email scanned copies to
[email protected]
. Step 3
: Wait for an email from the OUR for the results. Results of the evaluation will be released upon approval of the Dean of the Faculty of Study and the Registrar.

superadmin
2025-09-30T00:57:14+00:00
Application for Readmission
Readmission from Absence Without Official Leave (AWOL):
This refers to the process of allowing a student who has been absent without official leave (AWOL) to return to their studies. The authority to approve readmission from AWOL is delegated to the
University Registrar
. The process requires the student to apply to their respective Faculty, after which the Faculty endorses the application to the
Office of the University Registrar (OUR)
. Once approved, the OUR then updates the student’s status to
Active
. Note: AWOL status does not apply to students under the Professional Teaching Certificate (PTC) program. How to Apply for Readmission
Log in
to the
Student Portal
and click
Application for readmission
. Upload
the required documents along with the
proof of payment
of ₱225.00
Wait for the approval
of your application. Once approved,
proceed with enrollment.

---

## Application for Readmission – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/application-for-readmission/
**Chunk 1**

Applications received after the deadline will be processed for the next term, except for those from students with graduating status and those who are scheduled to take the comprehensive examination. If the student seeking readmission has exceeded the Maximum Residency Rule (MRR), they must submit a separate application for an extension or waiver of the MRR. Readmission also applies to the following:
Readmission from Dismissed Status:
This pertains to students who have been dismissed from their college or the University for reasons such as failing to meet retention requirements. The authority to approve readmission from dismissed status is delegated to the
Vice Chancellors for Academic Affairs (VCAA)
. Readmission from Permanent Disqualification (PDQ):
This is for students who have been permanently disqualified from the University. The authority to approve reinstatement/readmission for permanently disqualified students is delegated to the
Vice Chancellors for Academic Affairs

superadmin
2024-10-07T08:11:14+00:00
Application for Shifting of Program (Undergraduate Programs)
The shifting of program is applicable for undergraduate students of the Bachelor of Arts in Multimedia Studies (BAMS) and Bachelor of Education Studies (BES) programs who wish to shift to another undergraduate program of UPOU. Associate in Arts (AA) students who wish to pursue the BAMS or BES program must complete the AA program first then apply for admission to BAMS or BES; shifting is not allowed for AA to bachelor’s program. A current UPOU undergraduate student wishing to shift to his/her current program to another UPOU undergraduate program should have earned at least 18 academic units but not exceeding 36 academic units, depending on the program, with a GWA of 2.5 or better. Shifting of Program Policies:
Shifting of program is applicable only to continuing students who are within the Maximum Residency Rule (MRR) of the program.

---

## Application for Shifting of Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/application-for-shifting-of-program/
**Chunk 1**

A student on AWOL is not allowed to apply for transfer; he/she must be readmitted to his/her current program first. The student shall not be allowed to return to his/her previous program once transfer of program has been granted. Shifting of Program Requirements:
Accomplished
Application for Shifting of Program
form; and
True Copy of Grades (TCG). Note: TCG and TOR from former school/s can be requested
from the UPOU Office of the University Registrar. Go to the
Request Documents
page for more information. [Reference: Academic Policies and Procedures Manual]
How to apply for shifting of program:
Step 1:
Submit/Email the application requirements to your current Faculty of Study (FEd, FICS, or FMDS) two (2) weeks before the deadline for submission of application documents (refer to the
academic calendar
for deadlines). Step 2:
Wait for an email from admissions of the UPOU Office of the University Registrar notifying you of the result of your application. updated 29 January 2021

Application for Transfer of Program (Graduate Programs)
The transfer of program is applicable for current graduate students of UPOU who intend to transfer to another graduate program of UPOU. Students may not complete the graduate program before they transfer to another graduate program.

## Application for Transfer of Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/application-for-transfer-of-program/
**Chunk 1**

This does not apply to MENRM/MAS/MDE students who intend to exit through DENRM/GCAS/GCDE, respectively; they must consult the program chair or Faculty Office for exit instructions. Transfer of Program Policies
Transfer of program is applicable only to continuing students who are within the Maximum Residency Rule (MRR) of the program. A student on AWOL is not allowed to apply for transfer; he/she must be readmitted to his/her current program first. The student shall not be allowed to return to his/her previous program once transfer of program has been granted. Transfer of Program Requirements:
Accomplished
Application for Transfer of Program
form;
500-word essay stating the reason for transfer to the new program;
True Copy of Grades (TCG);
Admission exam (if applicable);
Other program-specific requirements. Note: TCG can be requested
from the UPOU Office of the University Registrar. Go to the
Request Documents
page for more information. [Reference: Academic Policies and Procedures Manual]
How to apply for transfer of program:
Step 1:
Submit/Email the application requirements to your current Faculty of Study (FEd, FICS, or FMDS) two (2) weeks before the deadline for submission of application documents (refer to the
academic calendar
for deadlines). Step 2:
Wait for an email from admissions of the UPOU Office of the University Registrar notifying you of the result of your application. posted 29 January 2021

Secure Faculty Clearance
Before applying for a University Clearance, the student must first secure a
Faculty Clearance
from their respective Faculty of Study. For FEd and FMDS students

Faculty of Study Clearance
Form
from the UPOU website. Submit the completed form via email to the appropriate address:

FMDS:
[email protected]

:
Accomplish the
FICS Faculty Clearance Form
online. Note: Failure to apply for Faculty Clearance will result in the delayed processing of University Clearance.

Application for University Clearance
superadmin
2025-09-29T06:09:14+00:00
Application for University Clearance
Who can file for a University Clearance? The following are the students who should file University Clearance*
Graduating students
Students withdrawing from the program
Students who will transfer to another university
Students applying for honorable dismissal
Students who will discontinue with their degree programs
*(
based on the UPOU Academic Policies and Procedures Manual, March 2021)
The  approved university clearance is a prerequisite for issuance of the following:
Official Transcript of Records
Diploma
H
onorable Dismissal
T
ransfer Credential
P
ermit to Transfer
Important Information:
If the student has no valid and complete Official Transcript of Records (OTR) from the previous school, the processing of the University Clearance will be on hold until the original OTR is submitted to the UPOU-OUR Records Section. Students must secure
Faculty Clearance
from their respective Faculty before applying for University Clearance. A student who has been cleared for the purpose of securing an
Honorable Dismissal
may no longer re-enroll in their previous program. However, they may apply for admission to another UPOU program through the Online Application System (OAS)
UNIVERSITY CLEARANCE PROCESS
Step 1
: Student applies for University Clearance through the
Student Portal
and submits necessary document/s. The document/s to be submitted depends on the reason for applying for a university clearance. Reasons
Document/s required
Graduate
Proof of payment for graduation fee of
300 pesos
. For payment procedures, click
here
.

---

## Application for University Clearance – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/application-for-university-clearance/
**Chunk 1**

To transfer to another UP Constituent University
Signed letter addressed to the University Registrar indicating the intent to transfer. Admission notice or acceptance letter from the accepting university. A government-issued ID. Withdrawal from the Program / Honorable Dismissal
Signed letter addressed to the University Registrar citing the reason/s for withdrawing from the program. A government-issued ID. Step 2
: Various UPOU units act on the submitted application for clearance. The clearance shall be only approved by the Office of the University Registrar (final approver) if the following units have cleared the student of any deficiency/ies:
Office of the Student Affairs
University Library
Faculty Office
Records Section of the Office of the University Registrar
Step 3
: Student receives email notification if the clearance submitted has been approved. Step 4:
Student downloads and prints the approved clearance form and certificate from the Student Portal. NOTE: Processing of the University Clearance takes 21 working days. It may be longer if the student has deficiency/ies in any of the above UPOU units. The student must settle all deficiency/ies first before a clearance is granted. Academic Procedures

## Enrollment Procedure – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/enrollment-procedure/
**Chunk 0**

Enrollment Procedure – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Enrollment Procedure
Home
/
Enrollment Procedure
Enrollment Procedure
superadmin
2026-04-06T05:39:08+00:00
Enrollment Procedure
Enrollment at UPOU is done using the online registration system (ORS) which can be accessed through the Student Portal. Only the following students are eligible to enroll at UP Open University:
Newly admitted students;
Continuing students;
Cross-Enrollees; and
Non-Degree students. For more information about enrollment at UPOU, go to the
Enrollment
page. Enrollment Guidelines:
Only students who are eligible to enroll are given access to the Online Registration System (ORS). The ORS is only accessible during the prescribed enrollment period; therefore, enrollment and payment of fees must be completed within this period. Refer to the
academic calendar
for enrollment schedules. All registration fees must be paid in full during the prescribed enrollment period. For scholarship grants and student loans, refer to the
Office of the Student Affairs
website for information. Important Reminders before Enrolling:
For first time enrollees:
Click on the “Request for Password” link on the homepage of the Student Portal and enter the student number assigned during admissions. Check your email for the system-generated password. Use this password when logging in to the Student Portal. For all enrollees:
Please make sure you are eligible to enroll and have sought academic advising before enrolling. Go to the
Course Offerings
page for the list of courses offered for the term. How to Enroll/Register Courses:
Step 1:
Log in to the
Student Portal
and access the Online Registration System (ORS). Step 2:
Select the term and select courses by clicking the course number/code. The system will automatically display the assessment of fees once courses are selected. Step 3:
Pay the registration fees through one of the
payment facilities
. Get a copy of the bank-validated slip/credit card transaction slip/official receipt. Upload a scanned copy/digital image of the payment slip to complete your enrollment. Step 4:
Wait for the confirmation of your enrollment.

---

Manual Enrollment Procedure
The manual enrollment is done after the prescribed enrollment period has lapsed. Only those with endorsement from the Program Chair are allowed to enroll manually. In some cases, manual enrollment is also applicable to the following students:
Students who are taking terminal courses, ex. Thesis, Dissertation, SP, etc.; and
Students who are under the Independent Learning mode of learning, i.e. Diploma in Science Teaching (DST) students. Requirements:
Accomplished google form for manual enrollment; and
Successful proof of payment for registration fees
View the
sample assessment computation
for the enrollment fees. The registration fee for Residency is Php 200.00. See
procedures for payment of fees
. How to Enroll/Register Courses Manually:
Step 1
: Accomplished the google form for manual enrollment (
Manual Enrollment Google Form
). Step 2
.

superadmin
2025-02-04T07:26:03+00:00
Request for Change / Correction of Student Information
Only the currently enrolled students can apply for a change of information which includes name, marital status, gender, and permanent address. The new information will be reflected on the student’s official transcript of records and diploma. Any request for a change of information must be made not later than the student’s last semester of enrollment in the university. Requirement:
Accomplished
Request for Change of Information Form
Procedure for Requesting Change/Correction of Student Information:
Step 1
: Submit the accomplished request for change of information form and a scanned copy of valid ID by email to
[email protected]
and attach the supporting document/s, such as PSA authenticated birth certificate, marriage certificate, and the like.

## Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/refund-of-fees/
**Chunk 0**

Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees
Home
/
Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees
Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees
superadmin
2024-10-07T08:04:30+00:00
Withdrawal of Enrollment and Refund of Fees
Refund of fees due to withdrawal of enrollment:
A student who has paid the matriculation fee but later on withdraws enrollment shall be entitled to a refund. The refund is based on the following provisions
If the withdrawal is done on or before the start of classes, the refund is 100%
If the withdrawal is done after the start of classes, the refund is 80%
Semester schedule – on or before one month after the start of classes
Trimester schedule – on or before three (3) weeks after the start of classes
NOTE: Only the tuition and miscellaneous fees shall be refunded. For the following cases, a request for refund/reimbursement of payment can be done anytime within the term that the payment was made:
Overpayment
Tuition fee exemptions and Reduced fee
Free tuition
Double payment
Dissolution of courses
Recipient of any scholarship grant
A payment intended for another UP constituent university that was sent erroneously sent to UPOU
Requirements to be submitted:
Letter addressed to the University Registrar (if the refund is due to withdrawal of enrollment)
The letter should state the reason/s for enrollment withdrawal. Accomplished Google form for refund request (
Google Form
)
Copy of proof of payment (enrollment payment)
Other supporting documents (e.g. authorization letter, approved scholarship grant, etc.)
Accomplished
Refund Application Form
How to apply for a Refund of Fees:
Step 1. Accomplish the
Refund Application Form
Step 2. Accomplish the
Google form
for refund requests. The application will not be processed if the required supporting documents are not submitted. Step 3
. The registration section (
[email protected]
) will email you in case there are clarifications that need to be addressed. Make sure that the provided email account is valid/functional. An invalid/non-functional email account will result to delay in receiving our reply. Step 4.

---

## Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only) – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/undergraduate-students-application-for-underloading/
**Chunk 0**

Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only) – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only)
Home
/
Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only)
Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only)
superadmin
2024-05-20T06:10:33+00:00
Undergraduate Student’s Application for Underloading (For BAMS and BES Students Only)
University of the Philippines Open University
s
tudents who are running for Latin Honors
should enroll in the prescribed load per term. As per the UP University Code (ART 414):
“Students who are candidates for graduation with honors must have taken during each semester/trimester/quarter not less than 15 units of credit load or the normal load prescribed in the curriculum in cases where such normal load  is less than 15 units unless the lighter load was due to justifiable causes such as health reasons, the unavailability of courses needed in the curriculum to complete the full loads, or the fact that the candidate is a working student.”
The regular load of a UPOU undergraduate student is 12 units per term, excluding PE and CWTS. To justify underloading under the conditions mentioned, the submission of pertinent documents/evidence is required:
For health reasons: submission of a medical certification from a government health facility (if the medical certificate is from a personal attending physician, the certification should be validated by a government health facility)
For unavailability of courses:  an approved plan of study and a written letter from the student as to why there is no course available or a certification from the PC that there is no available course available for the student
For employment reasons (Full-time employee renders an average of 40 hours per week): copies of appointment paper (which shall also indicate the duration of employment and job description), and certified true copy of payment slip. No more courses to take based on the curriculum:  an approved plan of study, and a written letter from the student stating that there is no course to take based on the curriculum or a certification from the PC that the student does not have any courses to take based on the program curriculum. If the reason/s for underloading is/are not any of the above and
if the applicant is NOT running for Latin Honors
, the applicant should submit a letter (addressed to the Faculty Dean) detailing the reason/s for underloading. How to apply for Underloading:
Step 1
: Log in to the
Student Portal
and click on Online Registration System (ORS) icon to start the enrollment process. If the ORS detects that the number of units you intend to enroll is less than the regular load, you shall be prompted to apply for an underload. Step 2:
In the ORS, click on application for Underload. Step 3
:  Choose from the dropdown list the reason for underloading. Step 4
: Upload supporting documents to justify your underload request. If you wish to edit your uploaded files, click on the “Edit” button (this is located beside the “Supporting Document” button). Step 5:
Click on the “Submit button” after finalizing the information/document needed for the application. Once your underload application is received by your Faculty Office, a system notification shall be emailed to you. Your application shall be acted upon in the following order:
Program Chair
Faculty Secretary
Dean
Step 6
: ORS will send notification about the status of the underloading application. If approved, log-in to your ORS account and continue with your enrollment. If disapproved, log in to your ORS account and follow the advice of your Program Chair (PC).

---

## Application for Overloading – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/application-for-overloading/
**Chunk 0**

Application for Overloading – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Application for Overloading
Home
/
Application for Overloading
Application for Overloading
superadmin
2024-05-20T05:56:45+00:00
Application for Overloading
For undergraduate students, the regular load is twelve(12) units, while for graduate students, it is six (6) units, except for MIS and DCS when there are school terms that their regular load is nine (9) units. If the student chooses to take more than the required units, he/she must request approval for overload from the faculty. How to apply for overloading:
Step 1
: Log in to the
Student Portal
and click on Online Registration System (ORS) icon to start the enrollment process. If the ORS detects that the number of units you intend to enroll is more than the regular load, ORS shall prompt you to apply for an overload. Step 2
: In the ORS, click on the application for Overload
Step 3
:  State the reason/s why you want to take additional courses and list down the additional course/s you wish to take. If you wish to update the list of courses you have chosen, click on the “Edit” button (this is beside the “Supporting Document” button). Step 4:
Click on the “Submit button” after finalizing the list of courses to enroll in. A system notification shall be emailed to you once your Faculty Office receives your overload application. Note that your application shall be acted upon in the following order:
Program Chair
Faculty Secretary
Dean
Step 5
: The ORS will send you a notification regarding the status of your request. If approved, log-in to your ORS account and continue with your enrollment. If disapproved, log in to your ORS account and follow the advice of your Program Chair (PC).

---

## Change Matriculation – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/change-matriculation/#
**Chunk 0**

Change Matriculation – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Change Matriculation
Home
/
Change Matriculation
Change Matriculation
superadmin
2025-10-06T04:06:49+00:00
Application for Change of Matriculation
Change of matriculation is the addition, cancellation, or change of a course/courses that is done after a student has officially registered. How to apply for a change of matriculation:
Step 1
: Log in to your
Student Portal
and click on
Application for Change of Matriculation
. Step 2:
Select the type of application applicable to you:
2.1. Cancellation of course(s)
2.2. Addition of course(s)
2.3. Combination of Cancellation and Addition of course(s)
Step 3
:  Select the reason for each application from the dropdown list. Step 4:
Click the “
Submit”
button after finalizing your application. Note:
You will receive a system notification once your Faculty Office has received your Change of Matriculation application. The application will then be processed in the following order:
Program Chair
Faculty Secretary
Dean
Step 5
: If your request is approved, click the “
Proceed to Payment”
button and settle the fees reflected in the system. If your request is disapproved, please email your Program Chair for clarification. Step 6:
Upload your Proof of Payment (POP) to the system. Step 7:
The Office of the University Registrar (OUR- Registration Section) verifies the validity of the payment. Step 8:
The OUR facilitates the request once the payment is verified as successful. Reminder to Students:
You can view the status of your application through your
AIMS Student Portal
account.

## Admission Policies – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/policy-admission/
**Chunk 0**

Admission Policies – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Admission Policies
Home
/
Admission Policies
Admission Policies
superadmin
2021-10-20T09:24:22+00:00
Admission Policy
No student shall be denied admission to the University System by reason of age, sex, nationality, religious belief, or political affiliations
[from Art. 332 of the UP System Code]. Any person seeking admission into a program must satisfy the minimum admission requirements specified in the program applied for. Relevant working experience may be considered in lieu of the minimum admission requirements by the Program Admission Committee
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. A student may transfer to the UPOU from another constituent university or vice-versa subject to his/her fulfillment of the requirements for admission as a transfer student by the university to which he/she is transferring
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. No person who has not duly matriculated may be admitted to the classes. In exceptional cases, the University Registrar may, on the recommendation of the Dean or Director concerned, authorize the admission of a visitor to a class for not more than five (5) sessions. [from Art.

---

## Admission Policies – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/policy-admission/
**Chunk 1**

335 of the UP System Code]. All other applicants who do not meet the minimum requirements specified in the program may be admitted as non-degree students
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. Academic Policies

2021-10-20T09:24:22+00:00
Admission Policy
No student shall be denied admission to the University System by reason of age, sex, nationality, religious belief, or political affiliations
[from Art. 332 of the UP System Code]. Any person seeking admission into a program must satisfy the minimum admission requirements specified in the program applied for. Relevant working experience may be considered in lieu of the minimum admission requirements by the Program Admission Committee
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. A student may transfer to the UPOU from another constituent university or vice-versa subject to his/her fulfillment of the requirements for admission as a transfer student by the university to which he/she is transferring
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. No person who has not duly matriculated may be admitted to the classes. In exceptional cases, the University Registrar may, on the recommendation of the Dean or Director concerned, authorize the admission of a visitor to a class for not more than five (5) sessions. [from Art.

## Credit Unit – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/credit-unit/
**Chunk 0**

Credit Unit – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Credit Unit
Home
/
Credit Unit
Credit Unit
superadmin
2021-10-20T09:24:57+00:00
Credit Unit
One credit unit is equivalent to one semester hour. A three-unit course therefore has a total of three hours of instruction per week, or a total of 48 hours of instruction per semester. In Distance Education (DE) mode, instruction includes self-study using self-learning instructional materials, as well tutorial sessions.

## Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC) – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/c/
**Chunk 0**

Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC) – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC)
Home
/
Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC)
Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC)
superadmin
2026-04-13T03:52:17+00:00
Crediting of Courses / Transfer of Credits (TOC)
For Undergraduate Students
For UP Transferees
Courses taken in another UP unit or program may be credited provided the content of these courses is equivalent to that of courses required for the UPOU program. Courses to be credited should not exceed 50% of the total number of units required for the UPOU program. For Non-UP Transferees
Courses taken in another university may be credited provided the content of these courses is equivalent to that of courses required for the UPOU program. Courses for which transfer of credit is being sought should have been taken within five years from the time of application for crediting. Courses to be credited should not exceed 50% of the total number of units required for the UPOU program. The student must take and pass a validation examination for every course for which transfer of credit is being sought. A validation examination can be taken only once. Validation exams will be scheduled as follows:
(a) before the first semester for first semester courses; and
(b) before the second semester for all other courses. For Graduate Students (Diploma/Master’s/PhD)
For UP Transferees
A maximum of 9 units earned in another UP unit or program may be eligible for transfer of credits. The final grades earned for these units must be no lower than 2.0. The courses for which transfer credit is sought must have been taken within the last five years from the time of application; otherwise, the student must take and pass validation exams. For Non-UP Transferees
A maximum of 9 units earned in another school, college or university may be eligible for transfer of credits. The final grades earned for these units must be no lower than 2.0. The courses for which transfer of credits is sought must have been taken within the last five years from the time of application. A student must take a validation exam for every course for which he/she is requesting transfer of credits. A validation exam for a course can be taken only once. The student must pass the validation exam for the course to be credited. A validation exam must be taken before or within the semester of admission.

Deferment of Admission
Deferment of Admission
superadmin
2021-10-20T09:25:29+00:00
Deferment of Admission
(Approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University)
An applicant is granted admission for a specific semester/term. If, for some reason, the applicant cannot enter the University in the term applied for, he/she may request deferment of admission to a later term. The request for deferment should be made not later than one month after the start of the term to which the student is first admitted. The start of the term refers to the first day of classes as stated in the official university calendar. Deferment of admission may be granted for a maximum of 12 months from the original term of admission. Applicants who wish to activate their deferred admission should write the University at least two months before the beginning of the term in which they wish to register, or at least two months before the end of the 12-month period of deferment to confirm their registration in the coming term. Failure to do so will mean having to re-apply for admission.

## Graduation with Honors – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/graduation-with-honors/
**Chunk 0**

Graduation with Honors – Office of the University Registrar
Skip to content
Graduation with Honors
Home
/
Graduation with Honors
Graduation with Honors
superadmin
2025-11-19T06:04:23+00:00
Graduation with Honors
(Revised UP Code: Art 410 as amended at 958th BOR Meeting: 24 Feb 1983)
Students who complete their courses with the following ABSOLUTE MINIMUM weighted average grade shall be graduated with honors:
Summa Cum Laude
—- 1.20
Magna Cum Laude
—- 1.45
Cum Laude
—- 1.75
Provided, that all the grades in all subjects prescribed in the curriculum, as well as subjects that qualify as electives, shall be included in the computation of the weighted average grade; provided, further, that in cases where the electives taken are more than those required in the program, the following procedure shall be used in selecting the electives to be included in the computation of the weighted average grade:
for students who did not shift programs, the required number of electives will be considered in chronological order;
for students who shifted from one (1) program to another, the electives to be considered shall be selected according to the following order of priority:
Electives taken in the program where the student is graduating will be selected in chronological order;
Electives taken in the previous program and acceptable as electives in the second program will be selected in chronological order;
Prescribed courses taken in the previous program but qualify as electives in the second program will be selected in chronological order. Students who are candidates for graduation with honors must have completed in the University at least 75% of the total number of academic units or hours for graduation and must have been in residence therein for at least two (2) years immediately prior to graduation
(Revised UP Code: Art. 412). In the computation of the final average of students who are candidates for graduation with honors, only resident credit shall be included
(Revised UP Code: Art. 413). Students found guilty of cheating/dishonesty shall be barred from graduating with honors, even if their weighted average is within the requirement for graduation with honors. Provided, further, that students who have been suspended for one (1) year or more due to conduct as defined in the Rules and Regulations on Student Conduct and Discipline; the Revised Rules and Regulations Governing Fraternities, Sororities and Other Student Organizations, and the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Anti-Sexual Harassment Act of 1995 shall be barred from graduating with honors. Students who are candidates for graduation with honors must have taken during each semester/ trimester not less than fifteen (15) units of credit or the normal load prescribed in the curriculum, unless the lighter load was due to justifiable causes such as health reasons, the unavailability of courses needed in the curriculum to complete the full load, or the fact that the candidate is a working student.

---

## Graduation with Honors – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/graduation-with-honors/
**Chunk 1**

(Revised UP Code: Art. 414 as amended at 884th BOR Meeting: 31 March 1977)
To justify underloading under the following conditions, the submission of pertinent documents is required
(Approved by UP President 18 October 1981)
:
For health reasons – medical certification to be confirmed by the University Health Service;
For unavailability of courses – certification by the major adviser and copy of schedule of classes; and
For employment – copy of payroll and appointment papers indicating among others, the duration of employment. For no more course to take based in the curriculum – approved plan of study (Approved by the UP Board of Regents at its 1379th Meeting on 3 April 2023)
It is the responsibility of the student to establish beyond reasonable doubt the veracity of the cause(s) of his/her light loading. It is required in this connection that documents submitted to establish the cause(s) of the light loading must be sworn to. These documents must be submitted during the semester of underloading. Reference:
UP Diliman Academic Information on Graduation with Honors

Home

Academic Load for Undergraduate and Graduate Students
Academic Load for Undergraduate and Graduate Students
superadmin
2021-10-20T09:20:47+00:00
Academic Load for Undergraduate and Graduate Students
The normal load for undergraduate students is twelve (12) units. Students who are running for Latin Honors should enroll at least twelve (12) units per term. The normal load for graduate students is six (6) to nine (9) units. Non-degree students may register for a total of 12 units only.

---

## Academic Load for Undergraduate and Graduate Students – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/academic-load-for-undergraduate-and-graduate-students/
**Chunk 1**

Regular students may choose to take on an academic load that is less than the normal prescribed load, provided doing so shall not prevent them from finishing their program within the time limit for completion of a degree. Students who opt for overloading should seek the approval of the Dean through the Program Chair, who must then endorse the said option to the Office of the University Registrar
(revised during the UP Open University Academic Policies and Procedures Workshop in April and May 2019). Academic Policies

Admission Policies
superadmin
2021-10-20T09:24:22+00:00
Admission Policy
No student shall be denied admission to the University System by reason of age, sex, nationality, religious belief, or political affiliations
[from Art. 332 of the UP System Code]. Any person seeking admission into a program must satisfy the minimum admission requirements specified in the program applied for. Relevant working experience may be considered in lieu of the minimum admission requirements by the Program Admission Committee
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. A student may transfer to the UPOU from another constituent university or vice-versa subject to his/her fulfillment of the requirements for admission as a transfer student by the university to which he/she is transferring
[approved during the 1st University Council Meeting of the University of the Philippines Open University]. No person who has not duly matriculated may be admitted to the classes. In exceptional cases, the University Registrar may, on the recommendation of the Dean or Director concerned, authorize the admission of a visitor to a class for not more than five (5) sessions. [from Art.

superadmin
2026-04-06T05:37:46+00:00
Admission to the Doctorate Program
The UP Open University offers 3 Doctorate programs, as follows:
(click on the program’s name for more information about the program and its curriculum)
Doctor of Communication (DCOMM)
Doctor of Philosophy in Education (PhD)
(Majors: Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Mathematics)
Doctor in Sustainability (DSus). Program Schedule/Offering:
Semester
Admission Schedule:
DCOMM
: every 1st semester of every academic year
PhD in Education:
1st and 2nd semester of every academic year
DSus:
1st and 2nd semester of every academic year
Admission Exam:
PhD in Education
: Not Applicable
DCOMM:
Not Applicable
DSus:
Not Applicable
Admission Qualifications and Requirements:
Admission Qualifications
Admission Requirements
Admission Qualifications
Admission Qualifications:
To be admitted to a Doctorate program, the applicant must:
have satisfied the program-specific requirements specified in the program’s information and curriculum. have submitted the complete documentary requirements for admission before the application deadline;
have taken and passed the admission exam if required for the program. Details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions; and
for PhD applicants, have completed the UPOU Distance Education Readiness Module (DERM). Link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Admission Requirements
Documentary Admission Requirements:
A. Documents to be mailed to the Office of the University Registrar:
1. A signed hard copy of the application form generated from the Online Application System. 2. Official Transcript of Records (OTR)
Reminders about the OTR:
Must bear the school’s dry seal and imprint, and the Registrar’s original ink signature. If multiple schools or degrees were attended/earned, submit the OTR from the most recent school, and this OTR must reflect all credentials from previous schools/degrees,
both completed and not completed degree programs
. 3. Two (2) Letters of Recommendation
These may come from an employer/supervisor, former professor, or former program adviser. Recommenders must use
UPOU Form 1a
. They can either mail it to:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
or they may email the recommendation, using their institutional email address, to
[email protected]
4. Original copy of birth certificate issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)
5. One (1) piece 2″ x 2″ ID photo (refer to the photo requirements below).

Should reflect all academic credentials from previous schools/degrees. 2. Essay
A 500-word essay stating your reason for applying to UPOU’s distance education programs and your plans after completing your graduate studies. 3. Proof of Payment of the Non-refundable Application Fee
PHP 500.00 for Filipino Citizens
USD 100.00 for non-Filipino Citizens
Payment instructions are available in the Online Application System. 4.

## Doctorate Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/doctorate-program/#tab-77a46466ec9f10e88ce
**Chunk 3**

NSO or PSA-issued Birth Certificate
5. Government-issued Identification Cards
6. Photo Requirements
Size: 2×2 inches
Color: Colored, with a neutral (white or off-white) background
Date Taken: Within the last 4 months
Face Visibility: Full face centered, eyes visible; no eyeglasses or accessories that obscure features
Labeling: Handwritten name tag in the format:
First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name, and Extension Name (if any)
, with signature over printed name
* Photos are AI generated
C. Additional Requirements for Foreign Applicants:
Passport and Birth Certificate
Copy of the passport’s data page (showing date and place of birth), and birth certificate or its equivalent, both authenticated by the Philippine Foreign Service Post. Upon admission to the university, the student must submit the following documents in hard copy:
Original Official Transcript of Records (OTR) with valid remarks. For details on what constitutes valid remarks, please visit:
TOR Acceptable Remarks – Office of the University Registrar
For applicants with international scholastic records:
Submit the original copy of the records, duly authenticated by the Philippine Foreign Service Post located in the applicant’s country of origin or legal residence. ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
Additional Requirements for the Doctorate Program
The additional documentary requirements must also be uploaded to the Online Application System. For portfolio and sample works, if the file size exceeds the limit (10MB) please upload a Word or PDF file containing the list of publicly accessible links (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox) where the required documents can be accessed. Additional requirements for Doctor of Communication (DCOMM)
Statement of Intent:
A 2,000-word essay outlining the applicant’s research plan, including the proposed research framework and problem (in question form) or topics of interest. Portfolio of Written Work:
A collection of the applicant’s written outputs such as publications, research studies (as sole or senior author), media productions, scripts, etc. English Language Proficiency:
Applicants who are non-native English speakers and did not complete prior academic coursework in English must submit proof of passing a valid English proficiency exam (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL). Computer and Internet Proficiency:
Demonstration of proficiency in using computer technologies, the Internet, and other ICT tools essential for independent research. Special Needs Statement:
A disclosure of any physical or mental conditions that may require special attention or support services. Additional requirements for PhD in Education
Educational Background:
A master’s degree in basic mathematics/science or a sub-field of Education (e.g., Science Education, Mathematics Education, Distance Education, Language and Literacy Education, Social Studies Education)
Alignment with Field of Specialization:
The master’s degree must include at least 15 units aligned with the PhD specialization. Otherwise, the applicant will be required to take supplementary 200-level courses in the chosen field. Academic Performance:
A general weighted average of at least “2.0” or “B” at the undergraduate level, or “1.75” or better at the master’s level. Leadership Potential:
Demonstrated through a statement of purpose, references, and other supporting documents. Sample Works:
Submission of at least two samples of completed creative or research work at the master’s level. Research Concept Paper:
A paper aligned with the intended dissertation topic. DE Readiness Module:
Completion of this module is required for non-UPOU graduates. Interview:
Applicants must demonstrate mental aptitude, language proficiency, and readiness for the program through an interview. Additional requirements for Doctor of Sustainability (DSus)
Leadership Potential:
Demonstrated through the statement of purpose, references, and other supporting documents. Scholarly Portfolio and/or Creative Work:
Includes scholarly or creative work (e.g., publications, policy papers, technical reports), a Curriculum Vitae, and a Certificate of Employment showing relevant work/research experience over the past 5 years. Dissertation Concept Proposal:
A 2,000-word document outlining the research problem and proposed framework. English Language Proficiency:
Required for applicants who are non-native English speakers and did not take prior academic coursework in English. Accepted exams include IELTS or TOEFL. Computer and Internet Proficiency:
Demonstration of capability to use ICT tools essential for independent research. Special Needs Statement:
Disclosure of any conditions requiring special attention or support. Midyear Intensive Program:
Attendance is required. Admission Procedure:
Step 1:
Apply for admission and upload the admission requirements through the
Online Application System
. Monitor the progress of your application through the OAS. Note:
If you are a UPOU alumnus or a former UPOU student applying for admission to a new degree program, please access the online application system through the
Student Portal
. Please do not create a new OAS account. Step 2:
As soon as the online application is accomplished, mail the
complete and valid
admission documents
to the address below:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
Refer to the UPOU Academic Calendar for the deadline for submission of application
documents. Step 3:
If required, take the admission exam. The details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions. Step 4:
If required, complete the DE Readiness Module (DERM). The link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Step 5:
Wait for an email from the OUR about the admission results.

Apply for admission and upload the admission requirements through the
Online Application System
. Monitor the progress of your application through the OAS. Note:
If you are a UPOU alumnus or a former UPOU student applying for admission to a new degree program, please access the online application system through the
Student Portal
. Please do not create a new OAS account. Step 2:
As soon as the online application is accomplished, mail the
complete and valid
admission documents
to the address below:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
Refer to the UPOU Academic Calendar for the deadline for submission of application
documents. Step 3:
If required, take the admission exam. The details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions. Step 4:
If required, complete the DE Readiness Module (DERM). The link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Step 5:
Wait for an email from the OUR about the admission results.

---

Doctorate Program – Office of the University Registrar

Doctorate Program
Home
/
Doctorate Program
Doctorate Program
superadmin
2026-04-06T05:37:46+00:00
Admission to the Doctorate Program
The UP Open University offers 3 Doctorate programs, as follows:
(click on the program’s name for more information about the program and its curriculum)
Doctor of Communication (DCOMM)
Doctor of Philosophy in Education (PhD)
(Majors: Biology, Chemistry, Physics, and Mathematics)
Doctor in Sustainability (DSus). Program Schedule/Offering:
Semester
Admission Schedule:
DCOMM
: every 1st semester of every academic year
PhD in Education:
1st and 2nd semester of every academic year
DSus:
1st and 2nd semester of every academic year
Admission Exam:
PhD in Education
: Not Applicable
DCOMM:
Not Applicable
DSus:
Not Applicable
Admission Qualifications and Requirements:
Admission Qualifications
Admission Requirements
Admission Qualifications
Admission Qualifications:
To be admitted to a Doctorate program, the applicant must:
have satisfied the program-specific requirements specified in the program’s information and curriculum. have submitted the complete documentary requirements for admission before the application deadline;
have taken and passed the admission exam if required for the program. Details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions; and
for PhD applicants, have completed the UPOU Distance Education Readiness Module (DERM). Link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Admission Requirements
Documentary Admission Requirements:
A. Documents to be mailed to the Office of the University Registrar:
1. A signed hard copy of the application form generated from the Online Application System. 2. Official Transcript of Records (OTR)
Reminders about the OTR:
Must bear the school’s dry seal and imprint, and the Registrar’s original ink signature. If multiple schools or degrees were attended/earned, submit the OTR from the most recent school, and this OTR must reflect all credentials from previous schools/degrees,
both completed and not completed degree programs
. 3. Two (2) Letters of Recommendation
These may come from an employer/supervisor, former professor, or former program adviser. Recommenders must use
UPOU Form 1a
. They can either mail it to:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
or they may email the recommendation, using their institutional email address, to
[email protected]
4. Original copy of birth certificate issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)
5. One (1) piece 2″ x 2″ ID photo (refer to the photo requirements below).

---

## Doctorate Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/doctorate-program/#tab-281f6a9f4ddc3152623
**Chunk 1**

B.

## Doctorate Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/doctorate-program/#tab-281f6a9f4ddc3152623
**Chunk 2**

Documents to be uploaded via the Online Application System:
1. Scanned Copy of the OTR
(Do not upload a photo of your TOR)
Should reflect all academic credentials from previous schools/degrees. 2. Essay
A 500-word essay stating your reason for applying to UPOU’s distance education programs and your plans after completing your graduate studies. 3. Proof of Payment of the Non-refundable Application Fee
PHP 500.00 for Filipino Citizens
USD 100.00 for non-Filipino Citizens
Payment instructions are available in the Online Application System. 4.

---

## Doctorate Program – Office of the University Registrar
**Source:** https://registrar.upou.edu.ph/doctorate-program/#tab-281f6a9f4ddc3152623
**Chunk 3**

NSO or PSA-issued Birth Certificate
5. Government-issued Identification Cards
6. Photo Requirements
Size: 2×2 inches
Color: Colored, with a neutral (white or off-white) background
Date Taken: Within the last 4 months
Face Visibility: Full face centered, eyes visible; no eyeglasses or accessories that obscure features
Labeling: Handwritten name tag in the format:
First Name, Middle Initial, Last Name, and Extension Name (if any)
, with signature over printed name
* Photos are AI generated
C. Additional Requirements for Foreign Applicants:
Passport and Birth Certificate
Copy of the passport’s data page (showing date and place of birth), and birth certificate or its equivalent, both authenticated by the Philippine Foreign Service Post. Upon admission to the university, the student must submit the following documents in hard copy:
Original Official Transcript of Records (OTR) with valid remarks. For details on what constitutes valid remarks, please visit:
TOR Acceptable Remarks – Office of the University Registrar
For applicants with international scholastic records:
Submit the original copy of the records, duly authenticated by the Philippine Foreign Service Post located in the applicant’s country of origin or legal residence. ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
Additional Requirements for the Doctorate Program
The additional documentary requirements must also be uploaded to the Online Application System. For portfolio and sample works, if the file size exceeds the limit (10MB) please upload a Word or PDF file containing the list of publicly accessible links (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox) where the required documents can be accessed. Additional requirements for Doctor of Communication (DCOMM)
Statement of Intent:
A 2,000-word essay outlining the applicant’s research plan, including the proposed research framework and problem (in question form) or topics of interest. Portfolio of Written Work:
A collection of the applicant’s written outputs such as publications, research studies (as sole or senior author), media productions, scripts, etc. English Language Proficiency:
Applicants who are non-native English speakers and did not complete prior academic coursework in English must submit proof of passing a valid English proficiency exam (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL). Computer and Internet Proficiency:
Demonstration of proficiency in using computer technologies, the Internet, and other ICT tools essential for independent research. Special Needs Statement:
A disclosure of any physical or mental conditions that may require special attention or support services. Additional requirements for PhD in Education
Educational Background:
A master’s degree in basic mathematics/science or a sub-field of Education (e.g., Science Education, Mathematics Education, Distance Education, Language and Literacy Education, Social Studies Education)
Alignment with Field of Specialization:
The master’s degree must include at least 15 units aligned with the PhD specialization. Otherwise, the applicant will be required to take supplementary 200-level courses in the chosen field. Academic Performance:
A general weighted average of at least “2.0” or “B” at the undergraduate level, or “1.75” or better at the master’s level. Leadership Potential:
Demonstrated through a statement of purpose, references, and other supporting documents. Sample Works:
Submission of at least two samples of completed creative or research work at the master’s level. Research Concept Paper:
A paper aligned with the intended dissertation topic. DE Readiness Module:
Completion of this module is required for non-UPOU graduates. Interview:
Applicants must demonstrate mental aptitude, language proficiency, and readiness for the program through an interview. Additional requirements for Doctor of Sustainability (DSus)
Leadership Potential:
Demonstrated through the statement of purpose, references, and other supporting documents. Scholarly Portfolio and/or Creative Work:
Includes scholarly or creative work (e.g., publications, policy papers, technical reports), a Curriculum Vitae, and a Certificate of Employment showing relevant work/research experience over the past 5 years. Dissertation Concept Proposal:
A 2,000-word document outlining the research problem and proposed framework. English Language Proficiency:
Required for applicants who are non-native English speakers and did not take prior academic coursework in English. Accepted exams include IELTS or TOEFL. Computer and Internet Proficiency:
Demonstration of capability to use ICT tools essential for independent research. Special Needs Statement:
Disclosure of any conditions requiring special attention or support. Midyear Intensive Program:
Attendance is required. Admission Procedure:
Step 1:
Apply for admission and upload the admission requirements through the
Online Application System
. Monitor the progress of your application through the OAS. Note:
If you are a UPOU alumnus or a former UPOU student applying for admission to a new degree program, please access the online application system through the
Student Portal
. Please do not create a new OAS account. Step 2:
As soon as the online application is accomplished, mail the
complete and valid
admission documents
to the address below:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
Refer to the UPOU Academic Calendar for the deadline for submission of application
documents. Step 3:
If required, take the admission exam. The details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions. Step 4:
If required, complete the DE Readiness Module (DERM). The link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Step 5:
Wait for an email from the OUR about the admission results.

---

superadmin
2026-04-06T05:49:08+00:00
Admission to the Master’s Program
The UP Open University offers 12 Master’s programs, as follows:
(click on the program’s name for more information about the program and its curriculum)
Master of ASEAN Studies (MAS)
Master of Arts in Language and Literacy Education (MALLE)
(for UPOU DLLE graduates only)
Master of Arts in Social Studies Education (MASSE)
(for UPOU DSSE graduates only)
Master of Arts in Nursing (MAN)
Master of Climate and Disaster Resilience (MCDR)
Master of Development Communication (MDC)
Master of Distance Education (MDE)
Master of Environment and Natural Resources Management (MENRM)
Master of Information Systems (MIS)
Master of International Health (MIH)
(for UPOU DIH graduates only)
Master of Land Valuation and Management (MLVM)
(for UPOU DLVM graduates only)
Master of Public Management (MPM)
Master of Research and Development Management (MR&DM)
Master of Social Work (MSW)
Program Schedule/Offering:
Trimester:
MDE
Semester :
All other Master’s programs
Admission Schedule:
MDE
: 1st, 2nd, and 3rd trimester of every academic year
MAS, MAN, MDC, MIS, MIH, MRDM:
every 1st semester of every academic year
All other Master’s programs :
1st and 2nd semester of every academic year
Admission Exam:
MPM
: MPM Graduate Admission Test (GAT)
(online)
MALLE
: MLLE Qualifying Exam
(online)
MIH
: MIH Qualifying Exam
(online)
MSW
: MSW Qualifying Exam
(online; to be taken by selected DSW graduates. List of examinees will come from the Faculty Office)
All other Master’s programs:
Not Applicable
Admission Qualifications and Requirements:
Admission Qualifications
Admission Requirements
Admission Qualifications
Admission Qualifications:
To be admitted to a Master’s program, the applicant must:
have satisfied the program-specific requirements specified in the program’s information and curriculum. have submitted the complete documentary requirements for admission before the application deadline;
have taken and passed the admission exam if required for the program. Details of and instructions for taking the exam will be emailed by admissions; and
for MDE applicants, have completed the UPOU Distance Education Readiness Module (DERM). Link to the DERM will be emailed by admissions. Admission Requirements
Documentary Admission Requirements:
A. Documents to be mailed to the Office of the University Registrar:
1. A signed hard copy of the application form generated from the Online Application System. 2. Official Transcript of Records (OTR)
Reminders about the OTR:
Must bear the school’s dry seal and imprint, and the Registrar’s original ink signature. If multiple schools or degrees were attended/earned, submit the OTR from the most recent school, and this OTR must reflect all credentials from previous schools/degrees,
both completed and not completed degree programs
. 3. Two (2) Letters of Recommendation
These may come from an employer/supervisor, former professor, or former program adviser. Recommenders must use
UPOU Form 1a
. They can either mail it to:
The Admission Section
Office of the University Registrar
UP Open University
Los Baños, Laguna 4031
Philippines
or they may email the recommendation, using their institutional email address, to
[email protected]
4. Original copy of birth certificate issued by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA)
5. One (1) piece 2″ x 2″ ID photo (refer to the photo requirements below).

A signed hard copy of the application form generated from the Online Application System (OAS). Mail it to the Admission Section, Office of the University Registrar
True copy of grades (TCG) for the Diploma program. This should be requested from the OUR-UPOU Records Section. A 500-word essay indicating your purpose for applying to the distance education programs and your plans after completion of graduate study at the UP Open University. This is to be uploaded in the OAS. Proof of Payment of Application fee
(non-refundable)
amounting to PHP 500.00 for applicants based in the Philippines, and USD 100.00 for applicants based abroad. Payment procedures are available in the Online Application System. Admission Procedure:

Philippines

documents. Step 3:

2nd Semester: 19 September 2026

For all the other programs

2nd Semester: 24 October 2026

for the Master’s Program

2nd Semester: 21 November 2026 (S)

1st Semester : 11 July 2026 (S)
